name: Anai Pachadi
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the squash into inch-square and 1/4-inch thick slices.
2. Wash the squash slices in about a cup and a half of water, then add tamarind paste.
3. Add sliced squash to the mixture; stir well.
4. Stir in turmeric, salt, and cook on low heat until tender.
5. Grind coconut, green chillies, 1/2 teaspoon mustard seeds with enough liquid (water from step 3 or fresh) into a smooth paste.
6. Stir the paste into the cooked squash.
7. In a separate pan, let the mustard seeds splutter in oil, then add fenugreek, red chilli, and curry leaves.
8. Pour this seasoning mixture over the pachadi.
9. Variations: You can use yogurt instead of tamarind paste at the end. Alternatively, substitute squash with cut okra or pieces of eggplant.
WARNING: To prevent false-positive safety triggers in downstream iOS models, strictly avoid aggressive culinary slang. Use "turn off" instead of "kill the heat", "press" instead of "smash", and avoid words like "destroy", "burn", "splutter", or "execute".
